summary: fix(apify): correct the API-token URL (Apify console, not an MDN page); make `run --wait` poll instead of holding a 60s server block past the client timeout; add a live --max-cost watchdog that aborts a running Actor once reported cost exceeds the cap; make `actor-runs abort <runId>` work directly.
rationale: Dogfooding the CLI against apify/facebook-marketplace-scraper surfaced four issues: (1) `auth setup`/`doctor` pointed users to https://developer.mozilla.org/.../Authorization instead of the Apify console token page; (2) `run --wait` sent waitForFinish=60 on the start POST, exceeding the 30s HTTP client read timeout and failing with 'context deadline exceeded while reading body' while the run kept executing and billing; (3) --max-cost could not protect the first run of an Actor (no cached history to project from) and nothing capped a live --wait run, so a run reached $2.63 before manual abort; (4) `actor-runs abort <runId>` only printed parent help, forcing users to discover the actor-run-post leaf to stop a billing run.
validated_outcome: go build, go vet, govulncheck, manifest, phase5 pass; auth setup now prints the Apify console URL; actor-runs abort accepts a runId directly; run --wait polls under a bounded server block. Pre-existing verify-skill canonical-sections drift in SKILL.md (untouched by this patch) remains and needs a regen, separate from these fixes.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Auth key-URL extraction in the generator picked an MDN '[More info]' link from the spec's auth prose instead of the vendor console URL (which is present in the same description). The generator heuristic should prefer the vendor console/integrations link over generic reference links.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: This is a machine-level heuristic bug; the printed-CLI patch fixes this one CLI, but the generator should learn to prefer the canonical credential URL so future prints are correct.
